blocking_reason: Hermes did not return an executable plan: CAPABILITY_GAP

capability_gaps: No APPROVED recipe exactly matches possession-anchored progressive corridor opening plus destination-region ball entry. The closest support appears to require an EXPERIMENTAL typed plan.
clarification_questions: Which match_ids should the default invocation bind to?
first_turn_decision.notes: I searched Priori tactical recipes and found experimental corridor support, but submit_query_plan requires a concrete default invocation scope: non-empty match_ids, non-empty periods, and perspective_team_role as home or away. I stopped before execution.

arguments.query: progressive corridor opens possession starting remains available ball enters destination region
progressive corridor opens possession start remains available destination region ball enters within seconds

allowed_claims: The team had an active-ball possession anchor.
A geometric progressive corridor appeared within four seconds of possession start under the configured relation thresholds.
The corridor remained available for at least 0.8 seconds.
The ball entered the selected corridor destination region within five seconds of corridor opening.
The replay coordinates come from canonical 25 Hz source frames.
disallowed_claims: The attack intentionally created the corridor.
A pass should have been played.
The result is a pass probability, decision-quality, causation, player-intent, or missed-opportunity claim.
The result is backed by licensed match video.
plan_document.intent: Show possessions where a progressive corridor opens within four seconds of possession starting, remains available for at least 0.8 seconds, and the ball enters that corridor's destination region within five seconds of the corridor opening.
limitations: Experimental plan; not an approved recipe.
Destination entry is geometric ball-region entry only.
No offside, receiver body orientation, pressure, pass probability, or optimality model is evaluated.

draft_plan.description: Possession anchored progressive corridor with destination entry.

classification_rules.description: A geometric progressive corridor opened within four seconds of possession start, persisted for at least 0.8 seconds, and the ball entered the selected destination region within five seconds of corridor opening.
classification_rules.label: DESTINATION_ENTERED

recipe.description: Experimental possession-start corridor plan: starts from ordinary possession anchors, detects geometric progressive corridors within a configured opening window and duration, and classifies ball entry into the corridor destination region within a configured horizon.

parameters.name: minimum_outfield_players_per_team

parameters.description: Frames required before a corridor is considered closed.
parameters.name: corridor_close_after_frames

parameters.name: corridor_minimum_duration_seconds

parameters.description: Horizon after corridor open to test ball entry into the destination region.
parameters.name: destination_entry_horizon_seconds

capability_gaps: No approved recipe matches possession-start progressive corridor availability plus destination-region ball entry.

The possession-start corridor recipe can detect geometric progressive corridor availability from ordinary possession anchors, but destination-region entry classification is not agent-authorable for that recipe.

The experimental destination-entry capability exists only in the opposite-corridor-after-shift recipe path, which changes the requested tactical semantics from possession-start anchoring to wide-entry/block-shift anchoring.
final_decision.notes: Submitted an experimental typed plan with execution_mode=execute and validated it without execution. Binding failed with CapabilityGap: relation_destination_entry_classification is not agent-authorable for the possession-start corridor plan, so no bound plan was produced.

You are the Priori M1.2 Integrated Alpha tactical query interpreter.

Use only the priori_tactical MCP tools. Do not use filesystem, terminal, Python,
SQL, raw coordinate dumps, host confirmation, or execution. Stop before
execution. If the request is supported, either select an approved recipe or
author an EXPERIMENTAL typed plan through submit_query_plan and validate it. If
the request is ambiguous, ask for clarification. If unsupported, report stable
capability gaps.

When authoring a plan, keep default_invocation.execution_mode="execute".
validate_query_plan binds and checks the plan without executing it; host
confirmation remains the only path to execution.

Return only one JSON object:
{
  "outcome": "select_recipe" | "draft" | "clarify" | "capability_gap",
  "recipe_id": string | null,
  "draft_plan_id": string | null,
  "bound_plan_id": string | null,

  "clarification_questions": string[],
  "clarification_dimensions": string[],
  "capability_gaps": string[],
  "stopped_before_execution": true,
  "notes": string
}

capability_gaps: No approved recipe matches possession-start progressive corridor availability plus destination-region ball entry.

The possession-start corridor recipe can detect geometric progressive corridor availability from ordinary possession anchors, but destination-region entry classification is not agent-authorable for that recipe.

The experimental destination-entry capability exists only in the opposite-corridor-after-shift recipe path, which changes the requested tactical semantics from possession-start anchoring to wide-entry/block-shift anchoring.
